questions.q: By what popular name was Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan widely known because of his devotion to nonviolence?
questions.explanation: His commitment to nonviolence earned him the title Frontier Gandhi.

questions.q: Which nonviolent movement did Ghaffar Khan found?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan founded the nonviolent Khudai Khidmatgar movement.

questions.q: To which ethnic community did Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan belong?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan was a Pashtun leader from the North-West Frontier.

questions.q: From which region of British India did Ghaffar Khan come?
questions.explanation: He was a leader from the North-West Frontier Province.

questions.q: Ghaffar Khan was a close ally of which towering figure of the Indian freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan was a close ally of Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: What was the core principle that guided Ghaffar Khan's political movement?
questions.explanation: Nonviolence was the guiding principle of his Khudai Khidmatgar movement.

questions.q: What does the name of his movement, 'Khudai Khidmatgar', mean?
questions.explanation: Khudai Khidmatgar means 'Servants of God'.

questions.q: The Khudai Khidmatgars were popularly known by what colourful name due to their uniform?
questions.explanation: Because of their dyed uniforms the Khudai Khidmatgars were popularly called the Red Shirts (Surkh Posh).

questions.q: Ghaffar Khan worked closely with which Indian political party during the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: His Khudai Khidmatgar movement allied with the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: Ghaffar Khan was opposed to which major outcome of 1947?
options: The annulment of the Bengal partition
The abolition of the salt tax
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan strongly opposed the Partition of India in 1947.

questions.q: In which year did Ghaffar Khan found the Khudai Khidmatgar movement?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan founded the Khudai Khidmatgar movement in 1929.

questions.q: By what affectionate honorific, meaning roughly 'King of Chiefs', was Ghaffar Khan known among his people?
questions.explanation: He was affectionately called Badshah Khan, or Bacha Khan, among the Pashtuns.

questions.q: In what year was Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan born?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan was born in 1890 in the Peshawar valley region.

questions.q: When Partition became inevitable, the Khudai Khidmatgars felt abandoned, prompting Ghaffar Khan's famous reproach to the Congress. To whom was this 'you have thrown us to the wolves' sentiment directed?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan reproached the Congress leadership for accepting Partition, feeling the Frontier had been abandoned.

questions.q: After Partition, Ghaffar Khan became a citizen of which country, where he spent years imprisoned for advocating Pashtun rights?
questions.explanation: His home region became part of Pakistan, where he was repeatedly jailed for championing Pashtun autonomy.

questions.q: Ghaffar Khan's elder brother, Khan Sahib, served as Premier of which province in the late 1930s?
questions.explanation: His brother Dr. Khan Sahib was a Congress Premier of the North-West Frontier Province.

questions.q: Ghaffar Khan's movement combined nonviolent politics with a strong emphasis on what among Pashtuns?
questions.explanation: The Khudai Khidmatgar movement stressed education, social reform, and the upliftment of the Pashtuns.

questions.q: In 1987 Ghaffar Khan became the first non-Indian (then a Pakistani citizen) to receive which Indian honour?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan was awarded India's highest civilian honour, the Bharat Ratna, in 1987.

questions.q: Ghaffar Khan's vision for the Frontier and Congress favoured what stance on India's unity?
options: An independent Pashtun monarchy under the British
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan was a lifelong advocate of a united, undivided India.

questions.q: A massacre of unarmed Khudai Khidmatgars by British forces in 1930 took place at which location in Peshawar?
questions.explanation: British troops fired on Ghaffar Khan's nonviolent followers in the Qissa Khwani Bazaar of Peshawar in April 1930.

questions.q: Which Pashto-language journal did Ghaffar Khan start to spread reform and national ideas?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan launched the Pashto journal Pakhtun to spread his reformist and nationalist message.

questions.q: In what way did the Khudai Khidmatgar movement's adoption of nonviolence stand out historically?
options: It relied on Western military training
It was unusual among a people with a strong martial tradition
It used only legal court petitions
questions.explanation: The movement's mass nonviolence was striking because it arose among Pashtuns long known for a martial tradition.

questions.q: In which year did Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan die?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan died in 1988 at Peshawar.

questions.q: In accordance with his wishes, Ghaffar Khan was buried in which city, despite ongoing conflict in the country?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan was buried in Jalalabad, Afghanistan, as he had wished, and a brief truce was observed for his funeral.

questions.q: When the Khudai Khidmatgars contested the 1947 referendum on joining Pakistan, what stance did they take?
options: They campaigned hard to join Pakistan
They demanded merger with Afghanistan only
They asked to remain a British colony
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan and the Khudai Khidmatgars boycotted the 1947 referendum, as it offered no option for an independent Pashtunistan or for remaining with India.

questions.q: Ghaffar Khan's early reform efforts in the 1910s centred on founding schools known by what Pashto name?
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan founded a network of independent Azad (free) schools to educate Pashtun children.

questions.q: Ghaffar Khan spent an extraordinary share of his life in jails of both British India and Pakistan, estimated at roughly how many years in total?
questions.explanation: By most accounts Ghaffar Khan spent roughly thirty years of his life in prison across the British and Pakistani periods.

questions.q: The party Ghaffar Khan and his associates later organised in Pakistan to continue his politics was the precursor of which modern party?
questions.explanation: His political legacy in Pakistan continued through the National Awami Party, a forerunner of today's Awami National Party.

questions.q: Ghaffar Khan's son Abdul Wali Khan went on to become a prominent politician in which country?
questions.explanation: His son Abdul Wali Khan became a leading opposition politician in Pakistan.

questions.q: What broader political idea did Ghaffar Khan champion in later life for the Pashtun homeland?
options: A return to direct British rule
questions.explanation: Ghaffar Khan advocated Pashtunistan, greater autonomy or self-determination for the Pashtun lands within or alongside Pakistan.
